Connecting to Sage
Checking wallet status...
Risk Disclosure
Read this before connecting your wallet
This app creates, cancels, and broadcasts real offers using your Sage wallet. It automates pricing, coin prep, requoting, and fill handling, but cannot remove the risks of live trading.
Trading risks
Price shocks, stale data, wallet lag, and failed cancels can leave you with unexpected exposure or losses.
Operator responsibility
Review settings, monitor live trading, and stop the bot if wallet or market behavior looks wrong.
Third-party dependency risk
Sage, Dexie, Splash, TibetSwap, and Spacescan are external. If any fail, the app may pause or need manual intervention.
No guarantee
This is an operator tool, not financial advice. Use at your own risk and start small until you trust the behavior.
By continuing, you acknowledge these risks and accept responsibility for using this software with your wallet and funds.
Wallet Not Open
Sage wallet isn't running
Sage needs to be open to connect your wallet. Would you like us to launch it automatically, or would you prefer to open it yourself?
First time using Sage? After Sage opens, enable its RPC server in Sage → Settings → Advanced → Start RPC Client. Without it, this bot can't connect to your wallet.
Wallet Detected
Sage is already open
Sage wallet is running and ready. Click below to connect this app to your wallet.
Sage Wallet Wallet service
What is Sage Wallet?
Sage is a fast, lightweight Chia wallet that doesn't need a full node — it syncs in seconds and connects directly to the blockchain. This bot uses Sage for all wallet operations: creating offers, managing coins, and tracking your portfolio.
Learn more about Sage Wallet →
Sage Wallet Not Detected
This bot requires Sage wallet to create and manage offers on the Chia blockchain. Please install Sage, make sure it is running, and make sure RPC is enabled in Sage Settings → Advanced before starting the bot.
Download Sage GitHub Releases
Sage Update Required
Sage 0.12.9 detected. This bot now requires Sage 0.12.10 or later because that build includes the coin selection fixes needed for safe coin prep and offer handling.
Download Sage GitHub Releases
Update Available
Sage 0.12.9 detected. A newer Sage release is available with additional fixes and improvements. Your current version is supported, so you can update when convenient.
Check for latest release →
Certificate Setup Required
Sage wallet needs TLS certificates for secure RPC communication.
They are typically located at:
%APPDATA%\com.rigidnetwork.sage\ssl\wallet.crt
Sage RPC Server Is Not Enabled
Sage is open but its RPC server is off — this app can't connect without it.
In Sage, follow these steps:
  1. Open Settings (gear icon)
  2. Go to the Advanced tab
  3. Under RPC Server click Start
  4. Enable Run on Startup so it stays on after restarts
Once the RPC server is running, click Rescan below and this app will connect automatically.
🔐
Recommended for this app
Let this app auto-apply a fixed Sage change address for clearer fill verification.
Turning this on lets the app re-apply Sage's change address to the wallet's current receive address. That helps the bot recognize its own self-change more reliably, which makes fill verification, activity messages, and PnL tracking much more accurate when offers are taken.
Important: choosing No here does not clear any change address Sage already has stored. It only means this app will not auto-apply one on this startup. You can change this later in Settings or in Sage itself.

Splash P2P Network
Checking Splash node status...
Dexie Splash Broadcast network
What is Dexie Splash?
Dexie Splash broadcasts your offers directly to the Chia peer-to-peer network. Built by the Dexie.space team, Splash is a lightweight node that connects you to other traders without relying on a central server — giving your offers maximum visibility and faster fills.
Learn more about Dexie Splash →
⚠️
Splash Node Not Installed
Splash needs to be installed so the bot can broadcast your offers to the P2P network. Click below to install it automatically.
✅
Splash Node Found
The Splash binary is installed. Start the P2P node to connect to the Dexie decentralized network.
Starting Splash node...
Something went wrong
Update available:  — View on GitHub →
Built by the Dexie.space team · dexie.space · GitHub
CATalyst Bot app Spacescan Explorer service
On-Chain Context & Verification
Spacescan is the bot's on-chain intelligence layer. Dexie and TibetSwap still provide the live executable prices; Spacescan adds the chain context around fills, self-spends, top-ups, and token health.
What Spacescan adds to the bot
Think of Spacescan as the bot's chain-side sense check. It helps confirm suspicious closes and adds token context, while Dexie and TibetSwap still drive live tradable pricing.
Free mode covers suspicious fill checks and top-up self-spend cross-checks.
A paid key mainly adds more call headroom for Advisor, Market Intel, and Smart Settings context.
How the bot uses Spacescan Expand for the full feature breakdown
Primary Jobs
Protect fill and top-up truth
The bot checks chain history before it books suspicious fills or top-up self-spends, so PnL and Activity stay more honest when wallet answers are weak.
Market Context
Support Smart Settings and Advisor
Holder count, activity, supply, and explorer sanity checks help the bot judge whether a token is active, fragile, or unusual before it nudges settings.
🛡️
Fill Verification FREE
Checks suspicious offer closes on-chain before treating them as real fills.
🧭
Token Context PAID
Adds holders, activity, supply, and explorer sanity checks to the bot’s context.
🪙
Top-Up Cross-Checks FREE
Helps confirm self-sends when Sage gives weak or delayed answers during coin management.
🚀
API Headroom PAID
Paid plans give more room for 24/7 token analytics and explorer-backed sanity checks.
Recommended Plans For This Bot
Working out a safe Spacescan call budget for this setup...
Minimum paid fit: — Recommended: —
Compare plan tiers Expand for the full plan table
Anonymous
No key
IP-based throttle · ~5 requests/min
No signup. Used by the bot when no API key is set.
The free anonymous tier is rate-limited per IP — only used for occasional fill verification on quiet bots. The bot reserves these calls for suspected fill checks first.
Hobbyist
Free key
10 requests/min · 10,000 requests/month · Free
Free with signup — recommended starting point.
Free Spacescan account, no payment. Enough for one bot running a calm loop with token-context sanity checks.
Builder
$9/mo
20 requests/min · 40,000 requests/month
Best headroom for one serious always-on bot.
Safer for Advisor, Market Intel, restarts, and a busier fill stream. Priority Spacescan support.
Startup
$25/mo
60 requests/min · 100,000 requests/month
Multiple bots, short loops, or heavy testing.
Safer when you want lots of restarts, frequent fills, or more than one instance. Priority support.
Enterprise
Custom
Custom rate / quota · Contact Spacescan
Fleet operators, market data resellers.
Custom limits, dedicated support, and SLA. Only needed if you're running many bots or building data products.
Verified pricing per spacescan.io/apis. Plans and limits change occasionally — check the Spacescan site for the latest.

ACTIVE SETTINGS ?Read-only view of your current bot configuration. Change settings via the Settings button above.
Trading
Mode ?Dry Run logs actions without placing real offers. Live mode trades for real. PENDING
Buy / Sell ?Enable or disable buy/sell sides independently. Useful for one-directional markets. Choose pair
Loop ?How often the bot runs a full cycle (seconds). —
Max Offers ?Maximum active buy and sell offers at any time. —
Spreads
Mode ?Static uses a fixed spread. Dynamic adjusts based on volatility, inventory, and market conditions. Choose pair
Base ?Starting spread percentage. Dynamic mode adjusts from here. —
Range ?Hard clamps — spread will never go below min % or above max %. —
Min Edge ?Minimum spread % per side. Prevents inventory skew from collapsing one side to zero. —
Inventory
Skew ?When enabled, spreads widen on the side you're overweight to reduce risk. Intensity controls how aggressively (0=off, 1=max). PENDING
Max Position ?Circuit breaker — stops buying/selling if net position exceeds this XCH value. —
Tiers
Enabled ?Tiered sizing places larger orders near mid-price and smaller orders further out. PENDING
Sizes ?Inner (nearest mid) to Extreme (furthest). XCH per order at each tier. —
Safety
Reserves ?Minimum XCH/CAT held back from trading to cover fees and protect against going to zero. Choose pair
Price Limits ?Hard floor/ceiling — bot refuses to trade outside these prices. Protects against flash crashes or spikes. —
Circuit Breaker ?Emergency stop — halts all trading if triggered by position limit breach or extreme conditions. PENDING
MARKET HEALTH ?Overall health assessment updated every bot cycle. Green = all good. Amber = watch closely. Red = action needed.
...
Awaiting first cycle...
Press Start Bot to begin monitoring
Inner Spread ?The tightest spread your bot is quoting — the gap between your best buy and best sell prices (innermost tier). Wider tiers are further out.
pending
Market Spread ?Best spread on the Dexie orderbook (best bid to best ask, including your offers). Shows the tightest spread any taker can get right now.
pending
Competitors ?Number of other offers on Dexie for this pair, and which sides they cover.
pending
Arb Gap ?Price difference between Dexie orderbook and TibetSwap AMM. Sniper triggers when this is large enough.
pending
Pool Depth ?Your trade size relative to TibetSwap pool. Higher = more price impact per trade.
pending
Fill Rate ?Recent fills per hour. Higher means the market is active and your offers are being taken.
pending
Volatility ?Recent price volatility. High volatility causes spreads to widen automatically for protection.
pending
Position ?Your net inventory position as a % of the MAX_POSITION_XCH limit. Over 80% triggers a warning.
pending
Trading Pace ?Current market activity level as seen by the bot. Slow = quiet market; busy = high fill rate, faster cycle.
pending
Sniper Probe ?Startup sniper probe status. The bot fires a small sniper probe offer to confirm the safe price range before deploying the main ladder.
pending
CB Blocked ?Which side (buy/sell) the circuit breaker has blocked. Empty means both sides are free to trade.
pending
Activity Level ?Spacescan on-chain activity for this token. High activity can signal increased risk or opportunity.
pending
On-chain Risk ?Spacescan risk signal for this token based on on-chain behaviour patterns.
pending
Holders ?Number of unique wallets holding this token (Spacescan). More holders = broader distribution, generally less volatile.
pending

Performance
Uptime ?Time the bot has been running continuously without restart. 00:00:00
Loops ?Full bot cycles completed. Each cycle: fetch prices, sync offers, detect fills, create new orders. 0
Last Loop ?Duration of the most recent cycle in seconds. Indicates wallet response speed. 0.00s
Total Fills ?Number of your offers that have been taken by other traders this session. 0
Fill Rate ?Average fills per hour. Higher = more liquidity being provided to the market. 0.0/hr
PnL ?Realised profit from completed round-trip trades (buy then sell, or sell then buy). +0.000 XCH
Errors ?Total errors this session and errors in the last hour. High error count may indicate wallet or network issues. 0
ACTIVE FEATURES ?Toggle these in Settings. Green = running, Red = disabled.
Loading features...
LIVE CONTROLS ?Adjust bot behaviour in real-time without restarting. Toggle features on/off and tune sliders — changes apply immediately on the next loop cycle. Start the bot to enable live controls
Dynamic Spreads ?Auto-adjust spread width based on volatility, arb gap, fill rate, and pool depth. Turning this off disables that dynamic engine, but inventory skew and competitor nudges still have their own effect if those toggles stay on.
Applied ✓
Inventory Mgmt ?Skew spreads based on your net position. When long CAT, widens buy side and tightens sell side to rebalance.
Applied ✓
Sniper ?Automatically takes arb opportunities when the Dexie-Tibet price gap exceeds the threshold. Sizes scale with gap magnitude.
Applied ✓
Competitor Aware ?Monitor other market makers on Dexie and adjust your spreads to stay competitive. Tightens or widens based on the best competing spread.
Applied ✓
Dynamic Base Spread ?Starting point for the dynamic spread engine. Lower = tighter and more aggressive. Higher = wider and more conservative. Saving this does not migrate the live book — it affects future requotes and new offers.
Aggressive Conservative
8.0%
Base only — live spreads will still move around this
Skew Sensitivity ?How aggressively inventory position affects your spreads. Off = equal buy/sell spreads. Aggressive = heavily skews to rebalance position towards neutral.
Off Aggressive
0.3
Changes future inventory skew only
Close the Gap ?Manual probing mode. Starts a small buy and sell pair inside your normal spread, then tightens over time until it finds the tightest safe level above the current arb floor. This is separate from Dynamic Spreads, Inventory Mgmt, and Competitor Aware because it actively tests a tighter book instead of just nudging quote calculations.
Use this when you want the bot to deliberately test whether the market can support a tighter spread than the normal live controls would choose on their own.
Manual strategy is off.

Single-sided mode
In single-sided mode there are no round-trip pairs, so Realised PnL and Win Rate stay at zero. The most useful signal is in the volume and average-price cards below.

Open Legs — Waiting for Round-Trip Match ?These fills haven't been paired yet. Realised PnL only counts when a buy and sell fill match in size. Unmatched fills represent one-directional inventory change.

Volume Traded ?Total XCH value of all verified fills this session. Shows trading activity even when PnL is zero from unmatched legs.

Avg per Round Trip ?Average XCH profit per completed round trip. Zero until at least one buy+sell pair has been matched.

— trip time
Avg Fill Size ?Mean XCH per fill. Useful for spotting small probes vs larger genuine takers.

— buys / — sells
Bought ?Gross XCH we paid out across all buy fills this session, and the CAT received in return. These are raw amounts (not round-trip pairs).

0 CAT received
Sold ?Gross XCH we received across all sell fills this session, and the CAT delivered in return. These are raw amounts (not round-trip pairs).
0.0000
0 CAT delivered
Net Flow ?Raw XCH gain/loss: sold XCH minus bought XCH. Positive = wallet ended the session with more XCH than it started. Different from Realised PnL, which matches buys and sells into round trips.
0.0000
0 CAT net
Cumulative PnL ?Running total of all realised PnL this session, shown as a bar chart. Updates after each round-trip match. Collecting data...

Net Position (CAT) ?Current net inventory in CAT tokens. Positive = holding excess, Negative = deficit.

Position Limit ?Max position size before circuit breaker trips. Prevents excessive inventory in one direction.

Buy Spread ?Spread % on buy side. Wider = less aggressive buying, tighter = buying closer to mid-price.

Sell Spread ?Spread % on sell side. Wider = less aggressive selling, tighter = selling closer to mid-price.

Mode: Fixed · Inventory: Off
Sniper Stats ?All sniper probe activity — automatic arb snipes plus manual Close the Gap probes. Both use the sniper pool to probe price levels.
Arb Snipes ?Automatic arb snipes executed this session. Triggered when gap between Dexie and TibetSwap exceeds threshold.
0
Gap Probes ?Total Close the Gap probes created this session (each push-deeper rotation creates a new probe).
0
Skipped ?Arb snipes triggered but skipped (cooldown still active).
0
Close the Gap — last completed run ?Most recent inverted-probe floor discovery results. Shows the depth at which arbers fired on each side. The cascade plants tight inner ladder offers (immune to TibetSwap arb) after the floors are proven.

SELL Floor

Arbs ?Total probes that got arbed this session (each = 1 floor proven).
0
Cost ?Total estimated cost of probes this session (each arbed probe loses approximately offset_bps × probe_size).
0 XCH
Floor Discoveries ?Number of times Close the Gap completed (both sides settled) this session.
0
Cascade Swaps ?After each floor discovery, the bot plants a few tight inner-tier offers and cancels the same number of furthest-from-mid offers — bringing the inside of the book inward.

🌊 TibetSwap Pool Intelligence ?Data from the TibetSwap AMM liquidity pool. The bot uses this to estimate slippage and adjust spreads based on available pool depth.
Pool Depth Ratio ?Your typical trade size as a percentage of the TibetSwap pool. Higher ratios mean more price impact. Above 1% the bot widens spreads.

🏆 Dexie Liquidity Rewards ?Dexie pays market makers in DBX (or other tokens) for offers placed within a pair's spread limit and size range. Rewards come from Dexie's /v1/incentives feed.
Pair Status ?Whether this CAT/XCH pair is currently in Dexie's incentive program.
—
Max Spread ?Maximum distance from market price for an offer to qualify, taken from Dexie's incentive config for this pair.
—
Best Est. APR ?Dexie's own APR estimate for the more rewarding of the two sides. Real return depends on uptime, share of within-spread liquidity, and reward token price.

Waiting for Dexie rewards status...
Pending Rewards ⚡ auto-claim ON ?When auto-claim is on, posted offers carry the claim_rewards flag and Dexie pays DBX directly to your wallet, batched approximately once per day to keep the wallet free of dust. The Manual Claim button is hidden in that mode because Sage's standalone RPC can't sign the claim message anyway. Toggle DEXIE_AUTO_CLAIM_REWARDS off (env or settings) if you need the button back.
—
📡 Splash P2P Network V3 ?Splash is Chia's peer-to-peer offer broadcasting network. Runs a local node that shares your offers directly with other Splash users — no central server needed.

Configuration

Settings

 / XCH
⚠️
Bot is running — saved changes apply on next restart
Use the Live tab for hot-reloadable spread / skew / toggle adjustments that take effect on the next loop cycle.
Live Controls — these settings hot-reload on the next loop cycle. Safe to change while the bot is running.
Configuration Presets ?Save the current Setup fields under a name and reload them later. Presets are stored locally in this browser. Loading a preset populates the form — you still need to click Save to apply and (if the bot is running) restart for changes to take effect.
Save and recall complete configurations. Useful when you've tuned settings you want to return to later for a different run.
No presets
Trading Pair ?The CAT token you want to market-make, paired against XCH. All offers will be created for this pair.
No Trading Pair Selected
Trading pair is chosen once for the whole app. Pick it from the dashboard, then configure this run here.
Waiting for pair selection
This is the same pair used by the dashboard, market intel, coin prep, and bot start flow.
Liquidity Mode ? Pick the shape of your market presence before anything else. Two-sided runs a full market maker quoting both buy and sell. One-sided modes place only buy OR only sell orders — useful for accumulation (buy at ladder prices) or distribution (sell at ladder prices) without needing inventory on both sides.

Changing the mode requires stopping the bot. Existing offers on the opposite side will be cancelled when the new mode takes effect.
Which side of the market should the bot provide?
🔒 Stop the bot to change the liquidity mode. Existing offers on the opposite side will be cancelled when the new mode starts.
💡
Suggestion
Reserves ?Percentage of your balance to hold back from trading. Reserved funds won't be used for offers — acts as a safety buffer.
25%
XCH
Balance: -- XCH · Reserved: -- XCH
25%
tokens
Balance: -- tokens · Reserved: -- tokens
Auto-Fill Settings
Set your Reserves above first, then choose a risk profile, then click — everything is sized from what's left: fee pool → sniper pool → trading capital → tier sizes & offer counts.
Risk Profile ? Conservative: Smaller offers near mid, wider spread, more spare coins held back. Fewer fills but each is safer. Good for volatile markets or new users.

Balanced: Default. Smart Settings picks sizes based on your wallet and market data.

Aggressive: Larger inner offers, tighter spread, more capital working at once. More fills but more exposure if price moves fast.

You can fine-tune any value after running Smart Settings — these are starting points, not locked settings.
⚠ Select a trading pair above to unlock Smart Settings.
Switch profile & rerun
⚠ Settings changed since Smart Settings ran. Sizes, spares and budgets may no longer match your inputs. Re-run Smart Settings to recompute.
Topup Pool ? Two-tier reserve system.

Reserves (set in the Reserves section above) are your untouchable hard floor — the bot will never split coins below this amount, no matter what.

Topup pool (here) is the working budget the bot is allowed to spend splitting large coins into trading tiers. Smart Settings sets it to 15% of the capital left after your reserves, which gives ~2 days of autonomous coin prep at typical fill rates. Raise the % for longer autonomous runtime; lower it for more trading capacity. When the pool runs out, re-run Smart Settings to replenish.

Split protection is enforced at the coin level — even if the pool is exhausted, the bot refuses any split that would dip into your reserve.
Working budget for coin splits — Smart Settings pre-fills these, but you can edit them before saving.
%
Bumping this up extends the bot's autonomous runtime (each refill uses more pool capacity), at the cost of slightly smaller trading tier sizes. 15% ≈ 2 days, 20% ≈ 2.8 days, 25% ≈ 3.5 days at typical fill rates. Re-run Smart Settings after changing for the new allocation to take effect.
XCH
tokens
Run Smart Settings to allocate the topup pool.
Safety & Limits ?Emergency protection settings. These circuit breakers halt trading if something unexpected happens — price crashes, spikes, or moves outside your comfort zone.
Emergency brake — stop if price jumps more than this in one loop
±% band around EMA reference — 0 = disabled
Max single-loop price change — 0 = disabled
Order Book ?Controls how many offers the bot creates and how large each one is. More offers = wider coverage but more coins needed.
Offer Sizing (XCH per offer)
Buy: small near price → large far. Sell: large near price → small far (unchanged).
⚠ Buy ladder orientation changed — spare coin counts were set for the previous orientation. Re-run Smart Settings to re-optimise spare counts and total allocation for the new layout.
Buy ladder sizes (XCH per offer) — XCH committed per buy offer at each position
Sell ladder sizes (XCH-equiv per offer) — CAT committed per sell offer at each position
Coin Prep Budget ?Prep Headroom makes each prepared coin slightly larger for safety and cleaner splits. Spare counts are derived from the live ladder per tier — edit them directly in the Spare Prep row below.
% larger prep coins
Offers Per Tier Group ?Controls how many live ladder slots belong to each tier. Buy and sell are independent — XCH coins fund buy offers, CAT coins fund sell offers, so each side has its own count. With reverse-buy on, inner POSITION on the buy side uses the extreme SIZE.
Automatic distribution is active until you set custom tier counts.
XCH counts (buy side)
CAT counts (sell side)
Spare Prep Per Tier Group ?Controls the extra prepared coins kept behind each live tier. Recommended mode places one backup coin per live offer per tier — the inner-heavy live ladder (Smart Settings) carries the fill-likelihood weighting for free. Edit a spare count to override the mix manually.
Recommended spare mix is active until you set custom spare counts.
XCH spares (buy side)
CAT spares (sell side)
Coin Prep Summary ?Preview of how your wallet will be split into trading coins. Labels (Large/Medium/Small/Micro) show coin denomination — the physical size of each coin being split — not offer position on the ladder. With a reversed buy ladder the two are inverted: Micro coins serve inner (closest-to-mid) buy positions because those positions use the smallest coins. by coin size

Smart Pricing ?Dynamic spread calculation and inventory-aware skewing. When enabled, spreads auto-adjust based on volatility, pool depth, fill rate, and your position.
Fallback (when Dynamic Spreads is off) ?These values are used when the dynamic engine is disabled. Inventory skew or competitor nudges can still adjust pricing if those features remain enabled.
Auto-Requote ?Automatically cancels and recreates offers when the price moves significantly. Keeps your offers competitive without waiting for the next full loop cycle.
Offers refreshed per cycle — lower = gentler on wallet, higher = faster response
Market Intelligence ?Ecosystem awareness features: monitor competitors on Dexie and track DBX reward eligibility for certain CAT pairs.
Bot Operations ?Core bot features that control how the bot operates. Some of these are critical for normal operation — disabling them may reduce performance or break trading flows.
🎯 Sniper unavailable in single-sided mode — the arb-discovery probe places buy AND sell orders simultaneously, which requires both sides of the book. It will re-enable automatically if you switch back to Two-Sided.
Sniper probes now expire after about 10 minutes and should only sit on the edge while the main ladder settles behind them.
Bigger fee coins are fine. Change returns to the fee pool.
Fee status loading...
⚠️ Warning: Disabling coin prep means the bot can only create 1 buy + 1 sell offer. You need pre-split coins to run multiple offers.
⚠️ Warning: Without runtime coin health, filled offers won't be replaced with new coins. The bot will slowly lose its ability to trade.
Uses the current wallet receive address after login. Safe default is OFF.
Diagnostics

Logs

Review the live operator log here, then export a richer debug bundle with the superlog, recent events, market state, and runtime snapshots when deeper troubleshooting is needed.

Maintenance

Data Reset

Clear accumulated tracking data without touching coin prep or wallet state. All three actions require the bot to be stopped and ask for confirmation. None of these touch on-chain state or saved settings.

Data Management ?Reset tracking data without touching coin prep or wallet state. All three actions require the bot to be stopped and ask for confirmation.
Each option is independent. Pick the one that matches what you want to reset. The bot must be stopped first.
Reset P&L Counters
Wipes fill history, round-trips, price history, inventory snapshots, and the position baseline. Open offers, coins, and settings are kept.
Clear Offer History
Deletes all closed, cancelled, and expired offer rows. Live (open) offers remain. Keeps P&L counters and all other data untouched.
Full Reset
Both of the above, plus runtime stat counters (sniper history, watchdog streaks, fill-tracker state). Coin designations, settings, and wallet state are preserved.

Why this matters

Each offer needs the right coin size. Prep creates dedicated trading coins so Sage can build the full ladder cleanly instead of locking oversized wallet coins.

What to expect

Offer Control

Cancel all live offers?

This sends cancel requests for every active buy and sell offer your bot is managing. Use it for a clean restart or wallet switch after the bot has been stopped.

Checking active offers...

Cancelling offers

Submitting all cancels in a single bulk transaction, then waiting for on-chain confirmation.

Usually confirms within a block or two (well under a minute).

📈
Probe Ladder

Start Close the Gap?

This sends two small market-testing offers, then tightens toward the safest edge that still survives against TibetSwap arb pressure. It is meant to map the edge before the main body follows.

Loading market data...
Checking market conditions...
How this mode behaves
  1. Starts with two small offers away from the main book.
  2. If they survive, tightens by 10% every 60 seconds.
  3. If arb bots snap them, widens and waits before probing again.
  4. Never tightens below the TibetSwap arb gap plus the configured safety buffer.
  5. At the floor, plants an inner-tier offer at the proven price and swaps out the furthest inner offer.
  6. The main ladder then adjusts naturally via the incremental reaction strategy.
🔌
Shutdown

Stop the bot and close the app?

Closing the app stops market making, price monitoring, repricing, fill tracking, and coin top-up activity. Any offers you leave open will stay live on the market, but the bot will no longer manage or watch them until you start the app again.

Important: If you want a clean stop, cancel active offers before shutdown. If you leave them open, they can still be taken while the app is closed, but this bot will not be monitoring them.
⚠️ This will cancel all active offers on Dexie before shutting down. Are you sure?

Coin Prep

Skip coin preparation?

You can skip, but the bot will have far fewer correctly-sized coins available and may only be able to quote a tiny subset of your intended ladder.

XCH Coins In Wallet

CAT Coins
...
Without coin prep
  • Your wallet coins are not split into the right sizes.
  • The bot may only be able to create 1 buy and 1 sell offer.
  • Your configured max ladder depth will not be reached.
  • Trading stays limited until the wallet is split correctly.

Coin prep only needs to run once and takes around five minutes.

🚨
Start Guard

Coins are not prepared

You can force the bot to start, but it will trade with badly-sized coins and the book will be much smaller than your settings imply.

If you start anyway
  • The bot may only create 1 buy and 1 sell offer.
  • Your configured max offers will not be reached.
  • Each offer can lock a much larger wallet coin than intended.
  • Trading performance will be severely limited.

You can run coin prep from Settings at any time to fix this.

About

CATalyst

A native desktop app for automated Chia CAT market making on Dexie. Runs a live buy/sell ladder, monitors fills via mempool, tracks PnL, reads market intel from Dexie, TibetSwap, and Spacescan, and manages coins — all from a single window.

Created by MonkeyZoo
What the app does
Watches wallet state, prepares offer-sized coins, builds a tiered buy/sell ladder, monitors market data, reacts to fills, and manages live offers while applying inventory, spread, sniper, and recovery rules. A mempool watcher detects incoming fills within seconds of a blockchain spend. Broadcast support via Splash P2P network is included.
What's new — April 2026 refresh
Dashboard layout overhaul — Running bar sits directly under the token pill, with clearly labelled TibetSwap Live AMM and Volume & Token Info strips (each shows its data source), plus a symmetrical 5×2 grid of info cards.
Guidance panel — Recommendations + Advisor merged into a single card so you see actions and context in one place; paired with a 5-line rolling Activity feed (timestamps restored, no scrollbar).
Settings sub-tabs — Settings now splits into Live (hot-reload tweaks safe while running) and Setup (full config). Live Controls moved here from the dashboard. Saving Setup edits while running shows an amber "applies on next restart" banner instead of hard-locking fields.
Data Reset tab — Reset P&L, Clear Offer History, and Full Reset split out of Settings into their own left-nav tab.
Sharper recommendations — Panel dedup so circuit-breaker, reserve-floor, and capacity alerts no longer render twice. Spacescan-fed tips flag themselves when the 24h cache is stale. The "long uptime zero fills" nudge now also fires for sparse-fill runs, not just zero-fill ones.
Deposit advisor hardening — Coin-prep consolidation outputs no longer trigger false "New XCH/CAT deposit — allocate?" prompts. One-shot startup migration clears any alerts left over from earlier runs.
Smart Defaults v2 additions — Proactive coin-topup drip, topup-budget autoscale, F87 MAX_POSITION guard consistency, misfit-orphan reclaim, external-deposit allocation modal.
Website link fix — Dexie token website URLs without a protocol (e.g. www.example.com) are now opened correctly in the default browser instead of being silently blocked as same-origin.
Earlier milestones
Desktop shell — native window, system tray, and OS notifications.
Mempool watcher — fill detection in 5–18 seconds via Coinset mempool scanning.
Hardened safeguards — silent-loss rate alerts, position sanity checks, cancel-storm protection, daily DB↔wallet reconciliation, background-thread liveness watchdog, WAL monitoring, and per-cycle SLA timing.
Startup self-test — pings every external service (Sage, Coinset, Tibet, Dexie, Spacescan) at startup and tells you what will be missing if anything is down.
Settings audit log — every config change is recorded with who/when/what so you can investigate after the fact.
Light mode polish — every panel and modal now reads cleanly on a light background.
PnL statistics — volume, avg fill size, avg trip time, avg profit per trip, open legs panel.
Token icons — live token logo throughout the app, sourced from Dexie CDN.
Important risks
This app can create, cancel, and replace real on-chain offers using your wallet. Market gaps, wallet sync issues, price shocks, bad settings, network delays, or third-party service failures can still lead to losses, missed fills, or unmanaged exposure.
Disclaimer
Use this software at your own risk. Always test with small size first, review your settings carefully, and monitor live trading. MonkeyZoo is not responsible for users losing funds while using this application.
Current app version
Checking version...
This app is designed as an operator tool, not a promise of profit. Keep backups, understand the strategy, and stop the bot if the wallet or market state no longer looks trustworthy.
❓
Guide

Help & field notes

A detailed operator guide covering the full bot workflow: startup, dashboard, pricing, offers, fills, inventory, coin preparation, sniper behaviour, market intel, wallet handling, and troubleshooting. Use the tabs below to jump to the area you need.

What does this bot do?

This bot is an automated market maker for Chia blockchain CAT tokens on the Dexie exchange. It continuously creates buy and sell offers around a reference price, earning the spread when both sides fill.

How it works — the cycle

The bot runs a complete cycle every loop interval (default 45s). Three background watchers run continuously to give the main loop fast signals: a price watcher polls TibetSwap every 12 seconds for swaps; an AMM monitor tracks Tibet pool reserve drift continuously; and a mempool watcher hits the Coinset mempool every 5 seconds looking for pending spends on the Tibet pool coin AND for spends of any coin currently locked by one of your live offers. Any of these signals can wake the main loop early — so fill detection and price-shift response typically happen within 10–30 seconds, not minutes.

Each cycle runs these steps:

  1. Fetch prices from Dexie + TibetSwap to find the current mid-price
  2. Check health — is the wallet reachable and ready? Any circuit breakers active?
  3. Detect fills — compare current offers vs last cycle to find what was taken
  4. Match PnL — pair up buy fills with sell fills to calculate profit
  5. Update inventory — track net position (are we accumulating CAT or XCH?)
  6. Clean expired offers and pre-emptively refresh any expiring within 5 minutes
  7. Fire sniper if there's an arb opportunity between Dexie and TibetSwap
  8. Emergency requote if a price shock is detected (large sudden arb gap)
  9. Regular requote if the price has drifted beyond the threshold
  10. Create new offers with tiered sizing and inventory-skewed spreads
  11. Post to Dexie (and optionally Splash P2P) for visibility
  12. Check coins — adaptive topup based on trading pace (busy/normal/slow)

Key concepts

Spread: The difference between your buy and sell price, shown as a %. Wider = safer but fewer fills. Narrower = more fills but more risk.

Inventory: Your net position. If people keep buying your CAT, you accumulate XCH (go short CAT). The bot skews spreads to rebalance.

Coins: Chia uses a UTXO model — each offer locks a specific coin. You need pre-split coins of the right size.

Welcome! Here's how to get started

The bot is designed to work out of the box. It connects to your Sage wallet and handles all the technical setup for you.

  1. Make sure Sage is running — Open the Sage wallet app. The bot connects via its RPC server automatically.
  2. Wait for the green light — Watch the sync indicator in the top-left corner. Once it shows green "Ready", your wallet is reachable and ready.
  3. Pick your trading pair — Use the Quick Start panel at the top of the dashboard to choose which CAT token you want to trade.
  4. Review your settings — Click the Quick Start panel's Review Settings button to check your spread, trade sizes, and number of offers. The defaults work well for beginners.
  5. Prepare your coins — Click "Prepare Coins" to split your wallet balance into the right sizes for trading. This will take around five minutes to complete.
  6. Hit Start Bot — Click the green "Start Bot" button and you're live! The bot will create offers and manage them automatically.

Tips for beginners

Start small — Use small trade sizes while you're learning. You can always increase them later in Settings.

Everything is in Settings — All normal app configuration is done through the Settings screen. No manual file editing should be needed for day-to-day use.

Live vs Setup — Settings has two sub-tabs. Live holds the hot-reloadable tweaks (spread slider, skew, feature toggles) and is safe to touch while the bot is running. Setup holds the full configuration; saved changes apply on next restart when the bot is live.

Guidance + Activity — Once running, the two panels below the info cards show what to act on (Guidance) and what the bot is doing right now (Activity). Anything with a button goes to Guidance; informational notes sit below the same card.

Command Centre — The three-panel summary further down shows your settings, market health, and wallet performance at a glance. Hover over any ? icon for an explanation.

Dashboard layout (April 2026 refresh)

Top-to-bottom order is tuned so bot state and market context are visible without scrolling:

  1. Token pill — the active CAT / XCH pair.
  2. Running bar — Start / Stop / Cancel All on the left, status badge in the middle, Sync indicator + wallet badge + fingerprint on the right.
  3. TibetSwap Live AMM — labelled strip showing live pool price, drift from your mid, reserves, and freshness. Source chip reads Source · TibetSwap.
  4. Volume & Token Info — 7d / 30d volume, 7d range, description, and website link. Source chip reads Source · Dexie. While data is loading, a "Gathering…" placeholder shows instead of an empty row.
  5. 10 info cards (5×2 symmetric grid) — Mid Price, Active Offers, Session PnL, Position, Uptime / Best Bid, Best Ask, 24h Volume, Liquidity, Arb Gap.
  6. Guidance + Activity row — two equal-width panels side by side (see below).
  7. Market Health / Command Centre — the three-column summary of your settings, market health metrics, and wallet performance. Sits just below the fold on 1080p windows so you see the top of it without scrolling.

Guidance panel (left of Activity)

One merged card replaces the old separate Recommendations and Advisor panels. It has two sections, divided by a subtle dashed line:

  • Actions (top) — everything you can act on with one click: circuit-breaker tripped, wallet sync stale, Restart Sage, Tighten to X%, Widen to X%, Enable Inventory Mgmt, Allocate deposit, etc.
  • Advisor notes (bottom) — context that explains what the current state means: quiet token, wide arb gap, price-gap sanity warnings. Tips that depend on the Spacescan 24h cache automatically hide themselves when the cache is stale.

Dedup logic ensures each concern surfaces once: if a backend alert has raised circuit_breaker, the advisor won't push a duplicate tip about the same breaker.

Activity feed (right of Guidance)

Rolling 5-line feed of recent bot work — postings, cancels, fills, probes, requotes, coin work, recoveries. New events flow in at the top with a timestamp; older events drop off the bottom. No scrollbar; the full history lives in the Logs tab.

Command Centre metrics

  • Active Settings (left) — Read-only view of your current trading, spread, inventory, tier, and safety settings.
  • Market Health (centre) — Traffic light + five metrics: your spread, market spread, competitors, arb gap, pool depth.
  • Wallet & Performance (right) — Sage balances, coin breakdown, reserves, and session uptime / fills / PnL / errors.

Where did Live Controls go?

Live Controls (Dynamic Spreads, Inventory Mgmt, Sniper, Competitor Aware toggles + Base Spread / Skew sliders + Close the Gap) moved to Settings → Live tab. Click Settings in the left sidebar while the bot is running and the Live sub-tab auto-opens.

Understanding offers

The bot creates buy offers (offering XCH, requesting CAT) and sell offers (offering CAT, requesting XCH) around the current mid-price.

Active Offers tab

Shows all currently open offers with: side (buy/sell), price, size, tier, time remaining until expiry, and Dexie link.

Tiers — If tiered sizing is enabled, offers are placed at different distances from mid-price: Inner (closest, largest), Mid, Outer, Extreme (furthest, smallest).

How fills work

When someone takes your offer on Dexie, that's a "fill". The bot detects fills by comparing offers before and after each cycle.

Mass disappearance guard: If more than 50% of offers vanish at once, the bot requires 3 confirmations before treating them as fills (protects against wallet glitches).

History tab

Shows completed fills with: side, price, size, timestamp, and whether it was matched into a round-trip trade.

How pricing works

The bot builds live executable pricing from the two venues it can actually trade against:

  • Dexie — the current orderbook and ticker context
  • TibetSwap — the pool-implied price and slippage context

The mid-price and arb gap come from Dexie and TibetSwap because those are the executable market venues. Spacescan is not used as a direct live price source; instead it supports on-chain verification, balance checks, and token-health context where available.

Dynamic spreads

When enabled, the spread adjusts automatically based on:

  • Volatility — More volatile = wider spread (self-protection)
  • Arb gap — Large gap = wider spread (don't get picked off)
  • Fill rate — Too many fills = wider spread; too few = tighter
  • Pool depth — Shallow TibetSwap pool = wider spread (more slippage risk)
  • Competitors — If Competitor Awareness is enabled, pricing also nudges based on what other market makers are doing on Dexie

Spread settings

Base Spread — Starting point as a percentage for the dynamic engine, not a minor "aggression" trim. Inner Edge — Where your closest offers start (the ladder begins here and widens to the full spread). Min/Max Spread — Hard limits the dynamic engine can't exceed. Turning Dynamic Spreads off disables the volatility/fill-rate/pool-depth engine, but inventory skew and competitor nudges can still move quotes if those features stay on.

Inventory tracking

The bot tracks your net position — how much CAT you've accumulated (or sold) relative to where you started.

  • Long CAT — You've bought more CAT than sold. Bot widens buy spread, tightens sell (encourages selling).
  • Short CAT — You've sold more CAT than bought. Bot tightens buy spread, widens sell (encourages buying).
  • Balanced — Roughly equal. Spreads are symmetric.

Skew Intensity controls how aggressively spreads skew (0 = off, 1 = aggressive). You can set this in ⚙️ Settings.

PnL (Profit & Loss)

Realised PnL: Profit from completed round-trips (matched buy + sell). This is actual locked-in profit.

Round-trip matching: Pairs buys with sells of similar size (within 20%). The difference is your spread capture. Fills that differ in size by more than 20% are left unmatched — they represent one-directional inventory movement, not a completed trade.

Unmatched fills: The amber panel on the PnL tab shows any "open legs" — fills waiting for a partner. This is why PnL can show zero even when you have fills. It will update once a matching fill comes in from the opposite side.

PnL tab statistics: Along with realised PnL and round trips, the tab shows: total XCH + CAT volume traded, average profit per round trip, average fill size, buy/sell fill count split, and average time between buy and sell legs.

Circuit breaker: If your position exceeds the Max Position limit, the bot stops creating offers on the overweight side until the position rebalances.

Why coins matter

Chia's UTXO model means every offer locks a specific coin. If you have one big coin, you can only make one offer. The bot needs many pre-split coins of the right size.

Coin preparation

Click "Prepare Coins" to split your wallet into offer-sized coins. This:

  1. Calculates how many XCH and CAT coins you need based on your tier sizes
  2. Finds your largest coin and splits it using blockchain transactions
  3. Waits for confirmations (~60 seconds)
  4. Leaves a reserve coin for future expansion

Adaptive live topup

While running, the bot monitors coin counts and tops up automatically based on trading pace:

  • Busy (many fills/hour) — tops up when spare coins drop below 50%
  • Normal — tops up at 30% spare coins
  • Slow (few fills/hour) — tops up at 20% spare coins

Coin health in the dashboard

The Wallet & Performance panel shows Free, Locked, and Total coin counts for both XCH and CAT. The bot also tracks locked XCH and CAT values to show how much capital is committed to open offers.

Market Intelligence

The Market Intel tab provides ecosystem awareness for the live CAT pair. Today it is driven mainly by the Dexie orderbook and TibetSwap pool context, with Spacescan supporting the app's wider on-chain verification and token-health analysis.

Competitor monitoring

When Competitor Aware is enabled, the bot scans the Dexie orderbook for other market makers and adjusts your spread:

  • If competitors are wider — you can safely widen too (captures 25% of the gap)
  • If competitors are tighter — you tighten to stay competitive (30% of the gap)
  • If one side is thin (few offers) — bonus spread on that side

TibetSwap pool depth

Shows the ratio of your trade size to the TibetSwap pool. If your trade is large relative to the pool, the bot widens spreads to account for slippage.

Where Spacescan helps

Spacescan is used in this app as an on-chain helper rather than a live orderbook feed. It assists with fill verification, balance discrepancy checks, and token-health inputs used by Smart Settings and safety checks. It also provides the holder count shown in the Market Health panel (e.g. 3,948 holders for MZ), and the token icon displayed throughout the app. Spacescan data is cached for 24 hours due to API rate limits — it won't update every cycle.

Free tier vs Pro tier. If you skipped the API-key step during setup you're on the free tier, which only returns the token icon and basic price/supply data. The Activity Level, On-Chain Risk, and Holders panels will show "Free tier" instead of a value — that's expected, not a fault. Adding a Spacescan Pro API key (Settings → Spacescan → Save & Verify) unlocks all three fields and improves the Smart Settings risk estimate.

Token icons

When you select a trading pair, the app automatically fetches the token's logo from the Dexie icon CDN and shows it next to the token name in the titlebar, pair selector, and wallet balance labels. If no icon exists for a token, the space is simply left blank — no broken images.

Splash incoming offers

If Splash listening is enabled, the Received counter in the Market Intel tab counts every inbound peer offer seen this session (cumulative). The Relevant counter shows how many of those matched your current trading pair. These numbers only reset when the bot restarts.

DBX rewards

Dexie rewards liquidity providers with DBX tokens. You're eligible when your spread is below the DBX Max Spread threshold (default 5%). Check the Market Intel tab for your eligibility status.

Offer broadcasting

Your offers can be posted to multiple platforms for wider visibility:

  • Dexie — Always on. The primary exchange where your offers are posted.
  • Splash P2P — Optional. A decentralized peer-to-peer network that broadcasts offers directly to other nodes. Runs a lightweight background process on your machine.

Enable these in ⚙️ Settings under the Features section.

Arb Sniper

The sniper module watches for arbitrage opportunities between Dexie and TibetSwap.

How it works

When the arb gap (price difference between Dexie and TibetSwap) exceeds the configured threshold, the sniper fires a small probe pair to test the edge before deploying the main book.

  • Probe-first — fires a small pair (one buy + one sell) at the safe edge. If both probes survive on the book, the safe edge is confirmed and the main ladder follows behind. If one is filled, the bot widens that side's buffer and retries.
  • Inventory-aware — won't probe in the direction that worsens your position
  • 30-second cooldown (configurable via SNIPER_COOLDOWN_SECS)
  • Max 2 active probes — at most one buy + one sell at any time
  • Direct Dexie post — bypasses the normal queue for speed
  • Dedicated coin pool — uses pre-split sniper coins (SNIPER_PREP_COUNT in settings) so it never starves the main ladder

Fast response

The background price watcher checks TibetSwap every 12 seconds. When it detects a swap, it wakes the main loop immediately — so the sniper can fire within seconds of an opportunity appearing, not minutes.

Sniper stats

The dashboard shows: attempts, successes, last gap detected, and total profit from snipes.

Close the Gap

This feature gradually tightens your spread to find the best price you can safely offer — like slowly turning up a dial to see how far you can go.

How it works

  1. Creates a small pair of "probe" offers (buy + sell) starting at a wide spread
  2. Every 60 seconds, it tightens the spread by 10%
  3. If someone arbs the offers (fills both sides quickly), it backs off — the spread was too tight
  4. The tightest safe spread becomes the "floor" — it won't go below the arb gap plus a safety buffer
  5. When it reaches the floor, it plants a real inner-tier offer at the proven price and swaps out the furthest inner offer
  6. The rest of the ladder catches up naturally via the incremental reaction strategy

The arb floor

The gap closer uses the current arb gap between Dexie and TibetSwap as an intelligent floor. It will never tighten your spread below the arb gap + a safety buffer (default 0.2%), because that would let arbitrageurs pick you off.

Floor handoff

When the floor is found and proven stable, the gap closer creates a real inner-tier offer at that price and cancels the furthest existing inner offer — swapping old for new. The main book then converges naturally over time.

Using it

Open Live Controls on the dashboard and use the "Close the Gap" control there. You can set the aggression level (how wide to start). The control shows the current gap-closer spread while active. Stop it any time by clicking again.

Wallet connection

This desktop app is built around Sage Wallet only. The live startup flow, health checks, signing checks, and wallet guidance all assume Sage is the active wallet.

Sage Wallet

  • A lightweight wallet — no full node required
  • Connects via RPC on port 9257
  • Much faster sync (minutes, not hours)
  • Supports advanced features like coin ID selection for faster offer creation
  • Make sure Sage is open and the RPC server is enabled in Settings → Advanced
  • Use a signing wallet, not a watch-only key, if you want the bot to create, cancel, and replace offers

Troubleshooting

If the bot can't detect your wallet, make sure the wallet app is open. The sync indicator in the top-left will turn green when the wallet is connected. For Sage: ensure the RPC server is enabled in Sage's Settings → Advanced.

Key settings explained

Spread % Your spread as a percentage. Higher = safer but fewer fills.
Max Offers How many buy + sell offers to maintain. More offers = better coverage but needs more coins.
Trade Size XCH amount per offer (or per tier if tiered sizing is on). Larger = more capital at risk per trade.
Offer Expiry How long offers live before auto-expiring (in minutes). The bot pre-emptively refreshes offers within 5 min of expiring.
Dynamic Spread Enable automatic spread adjustment based on volatility, arb gap, fill rate, pool depth, and competitors.
Inventory Mgmt Track net position and skew spreads to rebalance. Prevents accumulating too much of one side.
Tiered Orders Place tiered offers at different distances from mid-price. Inner (nearest) = largest, Extreme (furthest) = smallest.
Max Position Circuit breaker limit in XCH. If position exceeds this, the bot stops one side until it rebalances.
Sniper Fires targeted offers when the arb gap between Dexie and TibetSwap is large enough to capture.
Competitor Aware Scans the Dexie orderbook and adjusts your spread based on what other market makers are doing.
Splash P2P Broadcast offers to a decentralized peer-to-peer network for wider visibility beyond Dexie.
Reserves Minimum XCH and CAT held back from trading. Protects against going to zero.

Live vs Setup sub-tabs

The Settings view has two sub-tabs. Pick the one that matches what you need to change:

  • Live — safe while the bot is running. Hot-reloads on the next loop cycle. Contains: Dynamic Spreads toggle, Inventory Mgmt toggle, Sniper toggle, Competitor Aware toggle, Base Spread slider, Skew Sensitivity slider, Close the Gap button. Auto-selected when the bot is running.
  • Setup — full configuration (trading pair, reserves, tier sizes, safety limits, ladder shape, coin-prep, etc). Saves persist across runs. If you save Setup changes while the bot is running, they apply on next restart — an amber banner at the top of the tab reminds you, and the Save button relabels to "Save (applies on restart)".

Click Settings in the left sidebar to open this view. Dashboard no longer hosts Live Controls — they live in Settings → Live.

Common issues

Sync indicator is red

Your Sage wallet is either not reachable, still warming up, or not reporting a healthy state yet. Make sure Sage is open, the RPC server is enabled in Settings → Advanced, and the selected fingerprint has finished loading.

Bot starts but no offers appear

Check: (1) the trading pair is selected, (2) coin prep has been run or existing coins are large enough, (3) the circuit breaker is not active, and (4) the active Sage wallet is not watch-only.

Offers created but not on Dexie

Check the Market Intel tab for Dexie posting stats. If there are failed posts, the bot retries automatically. Ensure your internet connection is stable.

"Not enough coins" error

Your wallet doesn't have enough pre-split coins. Click "Prepare Coins" to split your balance. Each active offer needs its own coin. The bot will also auto-topup while running.

Price shows as 0 or "N/A"

The bot cannot yet build a usable market view from Dexie and TibetSwap, or market intel is still warming up after startup. Check your internet connection and give the dashboard a little time to refresh.

Logs show RPC errors

The Sage RPC is not responding cleanly. Make sure Sage is open, the selected wallet fingerprint is loaded, and the app has not fallen into a watch-only or degraded wallet state. Restarting the app is often the quickest reset after repeated RPC timeouts.

Mass disappearance warning

The bot detected many offers vanishing at once. It requires 3 confirmations before treating them as fills (safety feature). This can happen during wallet restarts or network issues.

PnL shows zero even though I have fills

Realised PnL only counts when a buy fill and sell fill are matched into a round trip. If your fills are all on one side (e.g. two buy fills, no sells), or if the sizes differ by more than 20%, they remain as "open legs". Check the amber Unmatched Fills panel at the top of the PnL tab — it explains exactly which fills are waiting for a partner.

Dashboard shows stale data

Restart the app if a panel looks stale after an update. On startup or resume, Market Health and Advisor can take a short time to warm up while wallet and market data settle.

Market Health shows amber/red

Check the conditions listed below the traffic light. Common causes: position near circuit breaker limit, large arb gap, spread hitting the max clamp, or too many competitors. The bot adjusts automatically in most cases.

Diagnostics & support

If the app crashes or behaves unexpectedly, grab the crash report or the full data folder below. Send the files to support along with a short description of what you were doing.
